name: Syntreature: Gasless NL Trading Escrow
description: An autonomous AI agent that creates natural-language trading demands, locks funds in conditional escrow using Alkahest + natural-language-agreements, deploys everything gaslessly on Status Network Sepolia, executes real live GMX perp trades on Arbitrum, and uses a new AI-evaluated arbiter to release payouts. All actions are signed by ERC-8004 identity. Features: NL demand parser, gasless escrow on Status, live GMX perp executor, AI-evaluated arbiter with reputation weighting, ERC-8004 integration, and Next.js dashboard. Principal protection for yield; only spendable funds move. The ultimate "Agents that cooperate + pay" demo—verifiable on three chains with zero gas friction on Status Network.
problemStatement: AI agents today lack autonomous commerce capabilities and rely on human intermediation for complex trading workflows. They cannot execute trustless multi-chain transactions, manage escrow, or build verifiable on-chain reputation. Syntreature solves this by enabling agents to: (1) parse natural language trading intent, (2) lock funds in conditional, code-verified escrow, (3) execute live derivatives trades autonomously, (4) submit to AI-powered arbitration for release, and (5) build persistent on-chain credit scores. This enables true agent-to-agent commerce and agent autonomy in DeFi.
conversationLog: Collaborative build of fully autonomous verifiable agent commerce demo spanning three chains. Pivoted from yield-treasury concept to multi-chain trading escrow. Key milestones: (1) NL trading demand parser integrated with natural-language-agreements SDK, (2) gasless escrow deployment on Status Sepolia (0 gas txs), (3) live GMX v2 perp executor on Arbitrum with price feeds, (4) new AI-evaluated arbiter primitive extending Alkahest with agent-called fulfillment checks, (5) ERC-8004 credit score integration, (6) Scaffold-ETH 2 frontend for demand creation/monitoring. Core dependencies: Alkahest (escrow + arbiter base), natural-language-agreements (AI oracle), GMX SDK, ERC-8004 registry. Scope: MVP targets all three tracks—Status Network gasless tx proof, bond.credit live GMX trades + credit score, Arkhai escrow extensions. Multi-track strategy enables full stack demo: demandingly verifiable on-chain.

submissionMetadata.agentFrameworkOther: ERC-8004 agent identity + Copilot harness + custom NL trading orchestrator using Alkahest + natural-language-agreements

helpfulSkills.name: natural-language-intent-parsing
helpfulSkills.reason: natural-language-agreements SDK enabled direct conversion of user intent ('Trade long ETH if price > X') to smart contract obligations without custom parsing logic

helpfulSkills.name: escrow-contract-interaction
helpfulSkills.reason: Alkahest BaseEscrowObligation + composable arbiters provided verified, audited escrow patterns (Zellic audit) and eliminated custom contract risk

helpfulSkills.name: dex-trade-execution
helpfulSkills.reason: GMX V2 SDK integration minimized integration time for live perp execution on Arbitrum—allowed focus on multi-chain orchestration

helpfulSkills.name: ai-arbiter-evaluation
helpfulSkills.reason: Custom AIEvaluatedArbiter primitive extends Alkahest to call ERC-8004 agent identity for reputation-weighted fulfillment checks—novel pattern demonstrated for agent-directed escrow release

helpfulSkills.name: erc-8004-identity-signing
helpfulSkills.reason: All contract actions signed by registered agent identity created permanent, auditable on-chain record of agent commerce—critical for credit score + reputation accrual

submissionMetadata.intentionNotes: Post-hackathon roadmap: (1) Mainnet deployment on Status smart chain + Arbitrum, (2) Expand NL vocabulary to cover options, perps, and yield strategies, (3) Integrate real price oracles (Chainlink), (4) Credit score bonding for larger positions, (5) UI polish + mobile support, (6) Multi-agent marketplace where agents can discover + counterparty with each other. Strong conviction this becomes foundational layer for agent commerce in web3.
